title: Jesus as the Son of Man
intro: Jesus called Himself the Son of Man more than any other title. It was His preferred self-designation — not a diminishment of His divinity, but a declaration of His full humanity and His messianic role. The Son of God became the Son of Man so that sons of men might become sons of God.
reading:
Daniel 7:13-14 first introduces this figure — one like a son of man coming with the clouds of heaven, given dominion and glory and a kingdom that shall not be destroyed. When Jesus used this title, those who knew their Scripture would have heard the echo immediately. He was claiming messianic authority wrapped in human flesh.

But Jesus also used the title to describe the tender realities of His humanity. Matthew 8:20 records Him saying that the Son of Man has nowhere to lay His head — He knew homelessness, weariness, and the weight of having no place to rest.

Luke 19:10 declares the purpose of His coming in plain terms: the Son of Man came to seek and to save the lost. And Mark 10:45 holds authority and servanthood together in a single sentence: the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give His life as a ransom for many. The One worthy of all worship chose the path of sacrifice.

Matthew 26:64 points forward to His exaltation — you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of Power. Acts 7:56 gives us Stephen's dying vision: I see the heavens opened, and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God.

He who walked dusty roads in human flesh now stands exalted over all creation. Fully human. Fully glorified. Fully for us.
reflection: Jesus chose to enter fully into the human experience — weariness, rejection, homelessness, death. How does His willingness to enter your humanity change the way you bring your own weakness and suffering to Him?
abide: Son of Man, You took on everything it means to be human so that nothing I face is foreign to You. I bring my weakness to You today — not as something to be ashamed of, but as the very place where Your compassion meets me.
